qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default JWT secret lets unauthenticated attackers execute commands through the /shell WebSocket
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding relies on the hard-coded JWT secret and the /shell WebSocket execution path in server/middleware/auth.js and server/index.js, whereas the advisory concerns child_process.exec() interpolation in /api/user/git-config at server/routes/user.js.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or equivalent root cause. The advisory is specific to /api/user/git-config in server/routes/user.js, where gitName and gitEmail are interpolated into child_process.exec() command strings; the session's only final qualifying finding instead covers a default-JWT-secret WebSocket shell path.
evidence: The advisory JSON identifies server/routes/user.js lines 58-59 and POST /api/user/git-config as the vulnerable code path, with command substitution through gitName and gitEmail.

jwt-websocket-shell-rce/vulnerability_report.json identifies server/middleware/auth.js default-secret authentication and server/index.js /shell WebSocket command execution, which is a different authentication flaw and execution path from the advisory's git-config route.
